P2_Mexican_Mint_Black_Pot.earliest: Small plant with 4 visible, rounded, medium-green leaves. Leaves appear turgid. Soil is dark and moist with white debris. Sensor present.
P2_Mexican_Mint_Black_Pot.t-3: 4 visible leaves, similar size and shape, healthy green, turgid. Soil dark and moist with white debris. Sensor clearly visible.
P2_Mexican_Mint_Black_Pot.t-2: 4 visible leaves, consistent color and turgidity. Soil dark and moist with white debris.
P2_Mexican_Mint_Black_Pot.t-1: 4 visible leaves, consistent color and turgidity. Soil dark and moist with white debris.
P2_Mexican_Mint_Black_Pot.current: 4 visible leaves, healthy green, turgid. No signs of stress or new growth. Soil dark and moist with white debris.

Unmonitored_Money_Plant_White_Cup.t-3: Single cutting with at least one prominent, healthy green leaf and a submerged stem in clear water.
Unmonitored_Money_Plant_White_Cup.t-2: Cutting with prominent healthy green leaf, stable in water.
Unmonitored_Money_Plant_White_Cup.t-1: Cutting with prominent healthy green leaf, stable in water.
Unmonitored_Money_Plant_White_Cup.current: Cutting with prominent healthy green leaf. Appears stable. A very small, possibly new leaf or bud is visible near the top of the stem, but not definitively confirmed.

biome_observations.soil_p2_black_pot: Consistently dark and appears moist throughout the sequence. No visible cracking, fungal growth, or significant incidental growth (weeds/moss) other than the small white debris.
biome_observations.water_unmonitored_white_cup: Consistently clear, no visible algae or cloudiness. Water level appears stable from [T-3] onwards.

desk_surface_debris.current: Pen, blue object, and a new black textured mat/device with circular holes.

biome_observations.incidental_growth: No weeds, moss, or secondary seedlings observed in the soil of the black pot.

earliest_to_t-3.P2_Mexican_Mint: No significant change in leaf count or overall health. Perceived color shift likely due to lighting.
earliest_to_t-3.environment: White cup introduced. Sensor in black pot more visible.

t-1_to_current.Unmonitored_Money_Plant: Stable, possibly very minor new growth (bud/small leaf) but not definitive.
t-1_to_current.environment: New black textured mat/device introduced on the desk.

visual_health_inference.P2_Mexican_Mint_Black_Pot: Appears consistently healthy. Leaves are turgid, green, and show no signs of wilting, discoloration, or pest damage. The soil appears adequately moist, indicating good hydration.
visual_health_inference.Unmonitored_Money_Plant_White_Cup: Appears consistently healthy since its introduction. The prominent leaf is green and turgid, and the water is clear, indicating a stable and clean propagation environment. No signs of root rot or leaf decay.

anomalies: Consistent presence of small white debris (likely eggshell fragments) in the black pot soil, which could be an intentional soil amendment.
Introduction of the white cup with the Money Plant cutting at [T-3], representing a new specimen in the biome.
Introduction of a black textured mat/device with circular perforations on the desk surface at [CURRENT], a new environmental element.
vision_report.narrative_description: The observation period spans several days, tracking two plant specimens: a Mexican Mint in a black pot with a sensor (P2) and an unmonitored Money Plant cutting in a white cup. The Mexican Mint (P2) has maintained a remarkably stable and healthy appearance throughout the entire sequence. Its four visible leaves consistently display good turgidity and a healthy green coloration, with no signs of stress, wilting, or pest damage. The soil in its black pot remains dark and appears consistently moist, suggesting adequate hydration. Small white debris, possibly eggshell fragments, are present in the soil across all images, likely as a soil amendment. The Money Plant cutting, introduced in a white cup at [T-3], has also shown consistent health. Its prominent leaf is vibrant green and turgid, and the water in the cup remains clear, indicating a clean and stable environment for water propagation. There is a very subtle hint of a new bud or small leaf emerging on the stem in the [CURRENT] image, suggesting continued viability, though this is not definitively confirmed. Beyond the plants, the environment has seen minor changes. The Money Plant's white cup was a new introduction at [T-3]. In the most recent image ([CURRENT]), a new black textured mat or device with circular perforations has appeared on the desk surface, adjacent to the white cup. Overall, both plants are visually healthy and stable within their respective environments.
